item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article was created for the article, and as such is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah. Makes it a lot more gut-wrenching.

He knows he's condemned to keep doing this, but also knows it's the only thing he CAN do now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late answer I know, but I believe he became resigned to his situation. Yes, he might be in hell, yes, there's something terribly creepy there with him, watching over his brief moments of awareness and yes, he might never be able to escape. But there's absolutely nothing he can do about it. That's the real strenght of the skip: The crushing realisation of inevitability

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how the name 'Bimmy' is a reference to the typo in Double Dragon III's intro.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading that last vid, I feel like Bimmy IS still aware of his situation, but he's decided to accept it for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better than the original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really sad, in a way that makes it powerful. His panic is really well-captured for such small segments showing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1'd!

This is a huge improvement over the last iteration. It sticks to one thing instead of being a weird mashup of "video what makes you want to kill the government" combined with "man making videos against his will." Now it's just one, and it makes it so much more focused and fleshed out.
Loving the cyclical nature of the videos, and, as previous commenters said, the contrast between the almost depressing videos and the stupid comments.

edit: oops this wasn't supposed to be a reply sorry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy and well-executed. Very classic "creepypasta" vibes. Especially love the (mostly) oblivious comment section, it really adds to the sense of helplessness. I feel like there's also some subtext about how unforgiving fans can be when real life gets in the way of a streamer's content, but that might just be me reading too much into things.

+1, nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still like this. I feel like it lost a lot of originality in the rewrite and is now mostly a retread of SCP-1733, SCP-2089, and SCP-1715, but the juxtaposition of Brian McKay's increasing fear and desperation against the inane cesspool that is the YouTube comments section offers enough for me to get behind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, pretty much did a complete rewrite based on criticisms! Thank you, everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a couple of issues I have with this. First of all, if the accounts consistently all have the same name then it would be easy to just pre-emptively block them from all relevant sites by either banning existing accounts and/or setting up dummy accounts with that name to prevent it from gaining access to the site. Of course the entitity could adapt to that, changing names, maybe even breaking into existing accounts, which could help to give the article more of a 'oh man, how do we stop this thing for good' vibe.

The whole assassination of US government members feels really unconnected to me and is just dropped out of nowhere. Apart from that it left me wondering what happens to somebody outside of the US if they watch one of the videos. You specify that people who can't find a government official instead turn to buildings, but those aren't exactly available either if you live in Europe, for example. A possible alternative to this, if you really want to keep the assassination part, would be to drop the focus on the US and instead have the affected people turn against their respective governments, depending on where they live. Anarchism instead of direct warfare, so to speak. Either way you need a better way to connect this to the story of the scip, so to not to drop it on the reader out of seemingly nowhere.

The dialogue of the videos feels kinda unnatural to me but I admit that may be because it's a reflection of how actual Let's Players talk, which often feels very forced and unnatural to me, too. Especially the erratic back and forth in the first log really threw me off while reading.

All in all I'd say you should take this back to the drawing board and try to forge a stronger connection between the two main parts of the article or, alternatively, drop the assassination part and focus more on developing the whole 'sentient program tries to make youtube videos' idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor quibbles for the author to consider, should rewrites be done:

the earliest has been recorded as seven seconds while the longest has been recorded as two hours and twenty six minutes

Did you perhaps intend "shortest" there, to contrast with the longest? It still works as is, as it would be describing the first known video as being seven seconds long. If that was intended, then disregard this bit.

Also, the various instances of "sat at a/his desk" should really be "sitting at a/his desk" to fit with the rest of the stage-setting descriptions being in present-progressive tense rather than past tense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoa, another internet based skip. The idea of an anomalous letsplayer definitely sounds interesting, but I'm having a hard time imagining why this guy wants his audience to kill political people.

So some suggestions if you really want your idea to pop:

- Try to think of the personality of this Bicameral dude. Make him interesting enough so that the reader will want to see this guy actually exist. (e.g. make us feel as if he's real and his channel is actually floating around on the internet somewhere)
- Capture the essence of lets play culture. Watch a few vids from some prominent lets players. What personality traits do they have that make them attractive to their audience? How is the community surrounding that lets player? You can get a good sense of this by reading the comments section on a youtube video.
- Also, since this dude is Russian, what is average Russian lets player like? What type of games do they play? (the only russian youtuber that comes to my mind is NFKRZ, and he is not really a letsplayer)
- Finally, try to think of the lets player motive. Why does he want his audience to kill people? And why is he doing it through lets play series?

Best of luck on your endeavors!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The scip is an interesting idea. And the idea that there's some anti U.S. Russian fellows are also interesting. However the execution needs more oomph.
No Vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Note:

In the event that an individual is determined to come under SCP-2391's effects to such a degree that amnestics would be ineffective are to be brought into custody immediately and kept in confinement until the effects have worn off.

Needs fixing.

All in all… I really wasn't sold throughout the description, but the dialogue was generally pretty compelling. ("Miss Lenora"' is a bit of a dumb name, though.)

I don't honestly know what it's lacking, but I feel it needs polishing. I guess the assassination thing feels pretty forced and discordant. -1, but I really hope you choose to rewrite it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know which eyes are mine - which are they?

I thought the "random scary" dialogue in the excerpts was rather good. Unfortunately the rest of the entry didn't live up to that standard - some clumsy phrasing, compulsion effect, note at the end (which could be deleted completely with no damage to the skip). And the overall idea of using new media to trigger sleeper agents is well-trodden territory - I don't feel like this adds much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not really feeling this one. There's not much mystery here and it's not scary. And if it's not mysterious or scary then what does it have? There's an idea here, but it's not skip material. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the masochistic guy (anyone remember the number of that one?)

SCP-2089 - /john/

Also, as author of 2352, I approve of that last bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really reminds me a lot of SCP-2762 when the last addendum came up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's been a couple variants on "weird youtuber", though…the big two that jump to mind are the masochistic guy (anyone remember the number of that one?) and SCP-2352.

This could still find a niche of it's own, but it needs to focus on developing the unique "subversive propaganda war via an unwilling mouthpiece" aspect.

Side note: if you can find a way to make this one work, Webcrawler J159DI ("That Weird Part of Youtube Again") could make a cameo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its an interesting set of Ideas, yet I don't feel like its fleshed out enough. The captured Youtuber idea might work better if its on its own

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with the above comments, this has some potential but not enough is done with the two primary elements to make anything of it.

e: Much improved after rewrite, whole article now feels cohesive and the logs are both crushingly depressing and terrifying. Bimmy comes across much more strongly and I empathized with his character, even if that character was a bit of a stock youtube personality. Downdoot changed to updoot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The two separate elements here (YouTube Let's Player converting people into killer agents and the Foundation creating an anomaly to turn over governments) are interesting but don't gel enough for me to support it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not feeling it. It's too… straightforward. No mystery. The dialogue isn't really sticking out from all of the other vague video logs I've seen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for telling me, I've fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this a let player? OMG I had an idea like this, this is cool! Can't wait to read it soon!

EDIT : Just letting you know Tanhony, your image format is really outdated.
